summary: Completed projection drift hardening: shared media select/join query builders now back both ID-batch and visible-media list paths, with shared MediaOut construction retained.
how_to_test: make migrate-test && cd python && DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/nexus_test uv run pytest -q tests/test_media_list.py tests/test_media.py::TestMediaListeningState tests/test_media.py::TestMediaListeningStateBatch tests/test_podcasts.py::TestPodcastApiSurface::test_get_podcast_episodes_supports_state_sort_search_and_derived_episode_state tests/test_podcasts.py::TestPodcastApiSurface::test_list_subscriptions_returns_unplayed_count_and_supports_sort_modes && DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/nexus_test_migrations uv run pytest -q tests/test_migrations.py::TestPodcastListeningStateMigration::test_head_contains_podcast_listening_state_table_contract && cd ../apps/web && npm run typecheck && npm test -- "src/app/api/media/media-routes.test.ts" "src/app/api/podcasts/podcasts-routes.test.ts" "src/app/(authenticated)/podcasts/podcasts-flows.test.tsx"
risks: Hydration logic drift is reduced by shared constructors, but large page sizes may still require profiling/tuning because capabilities and author payloads are assembled per row in Python.
Mark-all-as-played intentionally applies to currently visible rows (current filter/search/page), not full historical podcast corpus.
Subscriptions unplayed_count is computed live with joins/aggregates and may need index/tuning work for very large user subscription sets.
